The user wants me to run a specific bash command using curl with a Unix socket. Let me break down what this command does:

- `curl` - command-line tool for transferring data using URLs
- `-m 5` - maximum time in seconds allowed for the operation
- `-s` - silent mode (no progress meter or error information)
- `--unix-socket /tmp/crabgic-spike04-config-gFlPvF/uds-test.sock` - connect to the specified Unix domain socket instead of a TCP socket
- `http://localhost/ping` - the URL/path to request
- `; echo DONE` - after curl finishes, print "DONE"

I should use the Bash tool to execute this command.
